key_strengths: 80%+ recurring revenue from razor/blade consumables model across life sciences and diagnostics
34 consecutive years of FCF-to-net-income conversion above 100%, with $5.3B FCF at 21.4% margin
Danaher Business System (DBS) is a proven, difficult-to-replicate operating model refined over 40 years
Founding Rales family retains 13% ownership ($17B+), providing exceptional owner-operator alignment
Regulatory-driven switching costs in bioprocessing (Cytiva) and installed-base lock-in in diagnostics create a wide, durable moat
key_risks: ROIC at 5.8% is below the ~9% cost of capital, meaning value creation is currently negative on an economic profit basis
$9.9B Masimo acquisition adds leverage and integration risk in patient monitoring, outside Danaher's core competency
Bioprocessing recovery may disappoint — revenue has not recovered to 2022 levels three years later
Pro forma net debt could reach $20-23B post-Masimo, pushing leverage to 2.5-3.0x Net Debt/EBITDA
Adjusted EPS ($7.80) vs. GAAP EPS ($5.04) gap of 55% warrants ongoing scrutiny on earnings quality
red_flags: ROIC of 5.8% is well below estimated WACC of 9-10%
Forward EPS consensus ($9.11) exceeds management's own adjusted EPS guide ($8.35-$8.50)
Masimo had a turbulent pre-acquisition period including proxy fight and consumer electronics distraction
Serial acquisitions continuously reset goodwill base higher, keeping reported ROIC structurally depressed
buy_triggers: Stock declines to $140-$160 range, creating genuine margin of safety against bear-case intrinsic value
Two consecutive quarters of high-single-digit bioprocessing order growth confirming recovery thesis
Masimo closes cleanly with early DBS margin improvement within 2 quarters
sell_triggers: ROIC fails to recover above 8% within 3 years despite Masimo synergies
Another mega-acquisition (>$5B) announced before Masimo integration is proven
valuation_summary: At $187 (18.8x EV/EBITDA vs. 22x historical median, 20.6x forward P/E), Danaher is roughly fairly valued on base-case assumptions with base-case discounted value of ~$171 and bear-case discounted value of ~$116. The stock needs to fall 15-25% to $140-$160 for genuine Buffett-style margin of safety.
source_summary: Danaher Q4 2025 earnings release (Jan 28, 2026), Danaher 10-K, Yahoo Finance, MacroTrends, StockAnalysis.com, TIKR.com, GuruFocus, Quartr, Simply Wall St, Seeking Alpha, Masimo acquisition press release (Feb 17, 2026), In Practise (Cytiva moat analysis)
change_notes: Initial analysis. Key monitoring items: bioprocessing order recovery trajectory, Masimo acquisition close and early integration, ROIC recovery path, China VBP impact.
